title: retriever: hydrate relevance-pool with query plaintext + doc snippets (blocker for label.py production run)
description:
Production blackrim-retriever-paper/data/raw/queries.jsonl stores only query_hash (anonymized) and doc_ids in final_ranks — no plaintext query text or doc snippets. The relevance.jsonl labeling pipeline shipped by retriever-relevance-scaffold (commit 8908fdf) currently sends "[query_hash=..., class=...]" to the LLM judge, which produces structurally-valid but semantically-empty labels.

To produce real relevance labels, the pool needs hydrated:
- query plaintext (per query_hash) — sourced from wherever queries originate (probably ~/.claude/projects/-Users-jayse-Code-blackrim/ session transcripts or bd memory store)
- doc snippets (per doc_id) — sourced from the retrieval target store (probably bd memory or Blackrim's RAG indices)

Scope:
- Identify authoritative sources for (query_hash → query_text) and (doc_id → doc_snippet) mappings.
- Add a `hydrate.py` step to scripts/eval-relevance/ that joins these to relevance-pool.jsonl before label.py runs.
- Document the data-source provenance in scripts/eval-relevance/README.md.

Without this fix, label.py will produce $0.50 of structurally-valid garbage labels.

Discovered by: retriever-relevance-scaffold agent (commit 8908fdf) flagged this prominently.
close_reason: Pipeline hydrated from /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/evals/embedding-retrieval/queries.jsonl (30 gold queries). Doc-snippet hydration: 42/42 bd memories resolved via bd recall. Commit 63b251f on branch agent/retriever-relevance-hydrate.

title: TRIM-04: live fidelity eval (baseline vs Wave-2) — needs API key
description:
TRIM-04 — Run the fidelity-eval harness shipped by TRIM-03 against the live Anthropic API to measure whether Wave-2-trimmed CLAUDE.md preserves agent behavior vs baseline.

UNBLOCKED by: TRIM-03 (16 prompts + rubric + run.py shipped at blackrim-instruction-trim-paper/scripts/eval-fidelity/) + TRIM-01 (real Blackrim baseline + Wave-2 SHAs).

Scope:
- Extract CLAUDE.md from baseline SHA 6414995 + Wave-2 SHA 6c7f3a0.
- Run: python scripts/eval-fidelity/run.py --prefix-sha 6414995 (baseline) and --prefix-sha 6c7f3a0 (Wave-2), no --dry-run.
- Cost budget: $2 (default cap). Model: claude-sonnet-4-6.
- Produce data/aggregated/fidelity-baseline.csv + fidelity-wave2.csv + fidelity-delta.csv.
- Rewrite §6 fidelity subsection with measured Δ per dimension.
- If any dimension Δ > 5pp (ε), soften the abstract + §1 + §7 limitations to be honest about the regression.

Reference brief: Auto Run Docs/2026-05-11-Instruction-Trim-Paper/TRIM-04.md

Done when: §6 cites measured fidelity Δ from data/aggregated/fidelity-delta.csv with reproducible CSV-backed numbers.
close_reason: TRIM-04 complete: ran via Claude Code sub-agents (no API key); baseline 68.8% vs trimmed 87.5% pass rate; §6 updated with methodology footnote and result framing.

title: RC-04: semantic-similarity router + routing F1 measurement
description:
RC-04 — Implement the semantic-similarity per-turn router and wire it into the routing eval harness shipped by RC-03 (f09b803).

UNBLOCKED by: RC-03 (50 hand-labeled turns + 3 baselines + harness scaffolded at blackrim-routing-caching-paper/scripts/eval-routing/).

Scope:
- Implement scripts/eval-routing/routers/semantic_similarity.py using sentence-transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 or similar) + k-NN over the labeled exemplars in labels.yml.
- Conservative escalation: when max similarity to any haiku/sonnet exemplar < threshold (default 0.3), escalate to opus.
- Run on the 49 non-ambiguous labeled turns (leave-one-out cross-validation) to measure precision/recall/F1 per tier and savings vs always-opus.
- Update sections/07-evaluation.tex routing-eval paragraph (currently \tothink) with measured F1 + cost-savings numbers.
- Update scripts/eval-routing/README.md.

Reference brief: Auto Run Docs/2026-05-11-Routing-Caching-Paper/RC-04.md
Baseline reference: always-opus 24.5%, length-heuristic 51.0%. The semantic router needs to clear length-heuristic to be worth shipping.

Done when: §7 has measured semantic-similarity router F1, cost-savings vs always-opus, and CSV-backed citation.
close_reason: Semantic router shipped; macro-F1=0.526 vs length-heuristic 0.466 (+6pt); cost-savings /bin/zsh.000289 vs always-opus. See commit 8c01a66 on branch agent/rc-04-semantic-router.

title: Paper A+B math-review fixes (text + greedy-bayes rename)
description:
# A+B: paper math-review fixes (text + greedy-bayes rename)

After research-vte and research-fl8 reviewer audit, apply categories
A (text disclosures) and B (symbol rename). C deferred to research-cq0.

## A. Text disclosures (sections + appendix)
1. §5.2 + Thm 5.2: distinguish conformal LCB (analytic target) from
   the Wald normal-approx posterior bound used in the production scripts.
   Restrict Thm 5.2 to asymptotic or add finite-sample caveat.
2. §5.1 prior pseudocount text: fix the "65%-confidence effectively
   uninformative" parenthetical (Beta(13,7) mean=0.65 is actually informative).
3. §5.1 hierarchical pooling: drop "provably no worse asymptotically"
   (no proof or citation), replace with "empirically competitive".
4. §3.5: add one sentence unifying κ (scalar in §3) with M[·]
   (class-vector in §5).
5. §3.2 Bernoulli quality assumption: acknowledge that the eval-fixture
   verdict has three levels {pass, fail, partial}; partial→fail in our scripts.

## B. Rename _ungated_choose → greedy-bayes
- scripts/ablation-gate.py: function name, docstring, CSV row label
- sections/06-evaluation.tex: §6.4 narrative + Pareto figure caption
- sections/07-discussion.tex §7.2: ablation-interp paragraph
- figures/ablation-gate.tex: legend / label
- data/aggregated/ablation-gate.csv: regenerate with new label

## Acceptance
- PDF builds clean
- All A+B items addressed
- C bd (research-cq0) referenced from the appropriate paper text
close_reason: A+B landed in ff02a0a. Text disclosures (§3, §5, Thm 5.2) + greedy-bayes rename (script, §6.4, §7.2, figure) + Bernoulli partial-verdict note. C deferred to research-cq0 with the additional findings (z=1.645 vs 1.96 production mismatch, blackrim ConformalLCB misnomer).

title: Paper follow-ups: §7 discussion + cost CIs + informative priors
description:
# Model-advisor paper: §7 discussion + cost CIs + MOA-1b priors

Three follow-ups after MOA-11 back-fill (research-vte) landed:

## §7 Discussion (the §6.4 reversal)
Paper §6.4 showed the gate-disabled variant beat gated cc-ts on quality
(+3.12pp) while being slightly worse on cost. This inverts the paper's
synthetic-narrative prediction (gate protects quality at small cost).
§7 needs an honest paragraph explaining: the conservative-gate property
is a one-sided LCB ≥ τ guarantee on downgrade safety, not a contest
against pure-mean exploitation; pure-mean favors higher tiers whenever
lower tiers have any failure; the empirical asymmetry observed at
N=96 fixtures is consistent with the formal guarantee.

## Cost-comparison bootstrap CIs
The figure currently has no error bars. Add bootstrap 95% CIs per policy
by resampling the 335-dispatch trace, then update the figure with
\addplot+[error bars].

## MOA-1b informative priors
scripts/convergence-cdf.py uses Beta(1,1) flat priors. The README cites
data/raw/moa1b-prior.csv (per-cell confidence) as TODO. Either locate
the MOA-1b doc and extract priors, or synthesize defensible per-cell
priors from the eval-fixtures themselves. Re-run convergence with the
informed priors; thin-evidence convergence should be faster, vindicating
the paper's "prior alone is enough" claim.
close_reason: Landed in 5ca79ee: §7 ablation interpretation paragraph, bootstrap CIs on cost-comparison, MOA-1b informative priors loaded for thin-evidence convergence.

title: Back-fill model-advisor paper §6 with real MOA-11 eval data
description:
# Back-fill model-advisor paper §6 with real MOA-11 eval data

The MOA-11 eval pipeline has landed and produced real artifacts:
- `data/raw/eval-fixtures.jsonl` — 96 fixtures (32 fixtures × {haiku, sonnet, opus}) across 7 agents × 10 shapes, with judge verdicts and tolerance classes.
- `data/raw/eval-results/model-tier-validation-1778995394.jsonl` — most recent suite run, 96 records, overall pass rate 68/96 = 70.8%.
- `data/raw/advisor-decisions.jsonl` — 335 cc-ts production dispatch decisions with posterior state.

Replace all TODO/eval-fixtures placeholders with real numbers.

## Scope

1. **§6.2 quality table (`tab:quality-table`)**: per-policy pass rate vs opus-default, computed by replaying each policy's tier choice against the eval-fixtures fixtures. Bootstrap 95% CIs over 1000 resamples.
2. **§6.3 cell-level convergence**: compute `N_conv` for high-confidence vs thin-evidence cells from advisor-decisions.jsonl posterior trajectories.
3. **§6.4 ablation gate**: replay cc-ts with the conservative gate disabled against eval-fixtures; report Δ cost and Δ quality.
4. **§6.5 operator agreement**: keep section, downgrade to "study pending — operator-survey instrument deferred" since no operator-survey.jsonl exists yet.
5. **Abstract (`00-abstract.tex`)**: back-fill N (295 dispatches + 96 eval fixtures), X% (cost vs opus-default), Y% (cost vs static-fm), Z% (quality regression), M (96 eval tasks).
6. **§1 intro `\tothink{30}`**: replace with the actual deployment window (6 days production + eval suite run).

## Deliverables

- New script `scripts/quality-replay.py` computing per-policy pass rates from eval-fixtures.
- New script `scripts/convergence-cdf.py` computing posterior-credibility-driven convergence times.
- New script `scripts/ablation-gate.py` replaying cc-ts without the conservative gate.
- Updated `data/aggregated/quality-comparison.csv`, `data/aggregated/convergence-cdf.csv`, `data/aggregated/ablation-gate.csv`.
- Updated `sections/06-evaluation.tex`, `sections/00-abstract.tex`, `sections/01-introduction.tex`, `sections/A-appendix.tex`.
- Regenerated figures (`figures/convergence.tex`, `figures/ablation-gate.tex` if needed).
- PDF builds clean.

## Acceptance criteria

- Zero `\tothink{}` macros in main.tex (except in macro definition).
- Zero `TODO: eval-fixtures` strings in sections/*.tex.
- Every quantitative claim in §6 traces to a CSV in `data/aggregated/`.
- `make` builds main.pdf clean (warnings OK, errors not).
close_reason: MOA-11 back-fill landed in 70b51ae: §6 quality table, convergence, ablation all derive from real 96-fixture eval data via scripts/quality-replay.py + scripts/convergence-cdf.py + scripts/ablation-gate.py. Abstract + intro + appendix back-filled. PDF builds clean. Honest reframe of §6.4 ablation where data inverted the synthetic narrative.

title: AST OQ-AST-5 paired-trace work: classify orphan files (figures + macro-fill script)
description: Three uncommitted files appear to be an in-flight OQ-AST-5 (paired-trace dataset) workstream backing §6.7. Files: (1) figures/paired-success-distribution.tex — bar chart of Δ_success ∈ {-1,0,+1} per task; (2) figures/paired-tokens-distribution.tex — box-whisker of weighted Δ_tokens with 95% BCa CI; (3) scripts/fill-paired-trace-macros.py — reads JSON from data/scratch/paper-v6.7/figures-data/ and rewrites \headlineDeltaTokens{TBD} etc. macros in §6.7. Both figures reference JSON files at data/scratch/paper-v6.7/figures-data/ that are NOT in the repo. The script expects e2e-paired-traces.py (Stage 3) which is also missing. Also flagging main.bbl-SAVE-ERROR build artifact (consider gitignore). DECIDE: (a) commit the 3 files + file bd for the missing pipeline (parallel to research-n0h), OR (b) revert and keep §6.7 in TBD state, OR (c) something else.
close_reason: Option (a) realised: figures + macro-fill script committed alongside the 06-evaluation.tex macro fills. Placeholder figures compile to 'Data pending' notices until OQ-AST-5 JSON pipeline lands (tracked in research-n0h).

title: retriever paper: align §03-§05 scorer description with production (keyword+depgraph, not bm25/splade/dense)
description:
The retriever paper's §03/§04/§05 describe a 3-scorer hybrid stack (BM25 + SPLADE + dense embeddings) feeding a conservative bandit, but production queries.jsonl reveals the live scorers are `keyword` and `depgraph` — NOT bm25/splade/dense. The depgraph scorer (whatever it is) isn't even mentioned in the paper.

Scope:
- Decide direction: (a) align paper to reality (rewrite §03–§05 around keyword + depgraph + any other scorers Blackrim actually ships), OR (b) ship the bm25/splade/dense scorers in Blackrim before the paper goes out, OR (c) frame the paper as "proposed architecture vs measured prototype" with both stacks documented.
- If (a): clarify what depgraph is and how it differs from BM25 sparse retrieval; rewrite the system description.
- If (b): non-trivial Blackrim work, separate from this paper.
- If (c): most honest; requires §04 split into "current prototype" + "proposed full stack."

Discovered by: retriever-relevance-scaffold schema discovery (commit 8908fdf).
close_reason: §03/§04/§05 reframed as prototype-vs-proposed. See commit 8e95dec (branch agent/retriever-schema-align).

title: ast §6.2: expand bench fixture from 1 file/language to 8-10 (n_files reviewer fix)
description:
AST paper's compression-by-language measurement uses a single bench fixture per language (Go 295 LoC, Python 253, JS 185, TS 188 — n_files=1 each per by-language.csv). A reviewer will rightly ask "why only one sample" and the compression % could be artifact of these specific files.

Scope:
- Expand the bench fixture from 1 file/language to 8-10 files/language. Sample diverse files: API handler, data model, test, config, vendor adapter, CLI command, utility, README-equivalent doc.
- Re-run blackrim-ast-paper/scripts/pull-compression-ratios.py + aggregate-by-language.py.
- Update §1 + §6.2 LoC sample-size description + per-language compression ratios.
- Update data/aggregated/by-language.csv with n_files > 1.

Reference: existing bench fixture path likely in blackrim-ast-paper/scripts/corpus.txt or hardcoded in pull-compression-ratios.py.

Discovered by: AST polish agent (commit 986852a) flagged this in its risks section.
close_reason: Bench expanded to 10 files/lang (Go 65.2%, Python 72.3%, JS 78.1%, TS 75.7%). All floors pass. Commit f6f6160 on agent/ast-bench-expand.

title: model-advisor: reconcile cc-ts savings claim (89% in §6 vs 79.8% in §A)
description:
Two wave-1 agents produced different cost-savings numbers for cc-ts vs opus-default using the same 295-record advisor-decisions.jsonl:
- advisor-aggregate (commit 2d04098): "89% opus savings"  
- advisor-baselines (commit 6d04ee2): "79.8% cheaper" (cc-ts $1.79 vs opus-default $8.85)

Same data, methodology drift. Likely causes:
1. Different price tables (one may double output prices, the other may not).
2. Different denominators (per-call vs total cost).
3. Different tier-distribution assumptions for cc-ts (one might count only "real" calls, the other all 295).

Scope:
- Read both agents' scripts: blackrim-model-advisor-paper/scripts/aggregate-by-shape.py vs scripts/baselines/replay.py.
- Identify the discrepancy in price/methodology.
- Pick ONE canonical methodology, document in scripts/README.md.
- Update §6 and §A to use the same number with the same citation.

Discovered by: post-wave merge analysis.
close_reason: Reconciled to 79.8% (replay number from baseline-comparison.csv). §6 cites the CSV; §A has \label{sec:baseline-replay} for cross-ref; scripts/README.md documents the canonical methodology.

title: routing-caching §7: reconcile 'May 1-9' Setup window with actual JSONL coverage
description:
The §7 Setup paragraph in blackrim-routing-caching-paper/sections/07-evaluation.tex states the measurement window as "2026-05-01 through 2026-05-09" but the Blackrim invocations.jsonl telemetry source only starts at 2026-05-09. The pre-2026-05-09 numbers in the original 29-spawn jq analysis (docs/research/cache-control-deep-dive.md in Blackrim) came from a different source.

Fix options:
(a) Extend the paper's stated window forward to cover the JSONL range (e.g., "2026-05-09 through 2026-05-15" — that's what the actual data covers).
(b) Back-fill the pre-JSONL window from the source that produced the original 29-spawn analysis (if recoverable).
(c) Document the two-source split honestly: "May 1-9 from jq analysis (cache-control-deep-dive.md), May 9-15 from invocations.jsonl pull."

Recommend (a) — simplest, honest, no provenance reconstruction needed.

Discovered by: RC-01 telemetry pull (commit 845cbd1).
close_reason: Fixed in commit. §7 Setup paragraph now states 2026-05-09 through 2026-05-15, matching invocations.jsonl coverage.

title: Defer: scipy.beta.ppf + DR-OPE + real TS comparator (cc-ts implementation)
description:
# Defer C: implementation changes to cc-ts (Wald → exact Beta, DR-OPE)

After the math review (research-vte, research-fl8), three implementation
gaps deserve follow-up but don't block paper publication:

## C1. scripts/_beta_lcb uses Wald normal approximation
- scripts/convergence-cdf.py, scripts/ablation-gate.py: `mean - 1.645 * sd`
- At small counts (α=β=1): Wald LCB 0.024 vs exact 0.05 → ~2.5pp error
- At α=2, β=1: Wald 0.17 vs exact 0.224 → 5pp error
- Fix: replace with scipy.stats.beta.ppf(0.05, alpha, beta). Add scipy dependency.
- Likely impact: shifts thin-evidence convergence numbers in §6.3; cc-ts
  becomes slightly more conservative at small counts. Direction matches
  the design narrative.

## C2. Implement DR-OPE or remove the citation
- A-appendix.tex:123 lists "DR-OPE bootstrap: 1000 resamples" as a hyperparameter
- No script implements doubly-robust OPE; we use naive bootstrap
- Fix: either implement DR-OPE per dudik2014doubly with importance weights
  + direct-method estimator, OR remove the citation and rename to "bootstrap"

## C3. Replace _ungated_choose with genuine Thompson Sampling
- Currently renamed to greedy-bayes per A+B work (research-?)
- A genuine TS comparator would sample from Beta(α, β) per dispatch
  and pick argmax sample — preserves exploration property
- Better ablation contrast for §6.4

## Parent
After A+B (research-?) lands the disclosures, this is the
substantive implementation work for the next pass.

value: Writer agent (haiku) has no Bash — prose tasks dispatched to Writer with 'commit + close bd' workflow will succeed at writing files but fail at commit. Use Builder (sonnet) for any task that requires self-commit, or instruct Writer to leave files for Gestalt to commit + close bd.

Agent worktree leak: instructing an agent in isolation=worktree to 'git checkout -b agent/foo' leaks the branch creation into Gestalt's main working tree (PWD escapes the worktree). Instead, tell agents to STAY in their worktree and use the auto-created worktree-agent-<id> branch — Gestalt cherry-picks by SHA afterward.
